scope: Safe Drinking Water in Emergencies — extend fallback chain with chemical tablet and filter-based purification

librarySnapshot.note: No guides exist for chemical water purification tablets (iodine, chlorine dioxide, NaDCC) or filter-based purification (LifeStraw, Sawyer, hollow-fiber membranes). The fallback chain jumps directly from boiling to bleach. Bleach is currently positioned as tier-2 backup but should be last resort — this requires new constraint tags (no_tablets, no_filter) to gate it correctly.

currentChain.note: Good. Solid action_card, 7 steps, 4 warnings, 5 red flags, strong sources.
Currently tier 2. Should move to LAST RESORT once tablets and filter are added. Needs bleach-duplicate resolved and constraint update (add no_tablets, no_filter to constraintTags).
DUPLICATE of purify-water-bleach. Same role, same constraints. Should be archived.
Scenario guide. Surfaces when all chemical methods are off the table.

targetChain.note: EXISTING — update needed: add no_tablets, no_filter to constraintTags to push to last resort. Requires new constraint tags.

workItems.reason: No guide exists for chemical purification tablets (iodine, chlorine dioxide/Aquatabs, NaDCC). These are the primary backcountry and emergency kit alternative when boiling is not possible and bleach is unavailable. Common in go-bags and emergency supply lists. Should be tier-2 backup to boiling in the fallback chain.
workItems.guidanceNotes: Cover iodine tablets (not for pregnant women, thyroid conditions, shellfish allergy), chlorine dioxide tablets (Aquatabs, Potable Aqua CIO2 — preferred per WHO), NaDCC tablets. Include wait times, dosage, turbid water steps (pre-filter or double dose), storage/expiry. Key sources: EPA, WHO, CDC, Wilderness Medical Society.

workItems.reason: No guide exists for filter-based water purification (LifeStraw, Sawyer Squeeze, hollow-fiber membrane filters). These are increasingly common in emergency kits and are effective against bacteria and protozoa but NOT viruses — a critical safety distinction. Should be tier-2 backup to boiling.
workItems.guidanceNotes: Cover hollow-fiber membrane filters (LifeStraw, Sawyer Squeeze). Must clearly state: effective against bacteria + protozoa, NOT effective against viruses — in US/Canada backcountry this is usually acceptable; in international/flood contexts must combine with chemical treatment. Include backflushing for Sawyer, freeze damage warning, storage. Key sources: NSF/ANSI 58, EPA Guide to Drinking Water Treatment, CDC travelers' health.

workItems.reason: With tablets and filter added as tier-2 options, bleach must move to last resort. This requires adding no_tablets and no_filter to its constraintTags so the mobile chat only surfaces it when the user has confirmed tablets and filter are also unavailable. Requires new constraint tags (see proposedNewTags).

proposedNewTags.note: Two new constraint tags are required to correctly gate bleach as last resort. These must be added to constraint-tags.ts (PrepKit Admin/prepkit-admin/src/lib/constants/constraint-tags.ts) before the constraint annotation step can be finalized. This is a proposed code change — not applied during dry run.

tags.description: User does not have water purification tablets available (iodine, chlorine dioxide, NaDCC)
User does not have a water filter available (LifeStraw, Sawyer, hollow-fiber membrane filter)

duplicateRisks.overlapDescription: Both are action_cards in 'Safe Drinking Water in Emergencies' covering household bleach disinfection. Both have role=backup, same constraintTags, same blockedBy. purify-water-bleach has 7 steps and 3 source refs; purifying-water-with-household-liquid-bleach has 6 steps and 3 source refs. Near-identical coverage.
duplicateRisks.recommendation: Archive purifying-water-with-household-liquid-bleach. Its better step details were already ported to purify-water-bleach during the bleach consolidation run. The duplicate should not remain as a second 'backup' with identical metadata — it produces conflicting results in the mobile chat fallback chain.
